_instructions: This file defines the SharePoint sites to be created.
Add, remove, or modify sites as needed.
Each site requires: name, display_name, description

sites.name: executive-leadership
sites.description: Executive team communications, board materials, and strategic planning documents

sites.name: human-resources
sites.description: HR policies, employee handbook, benefits information, and recruitment materials

sites.name: finance-department
sites.description: Financial reports, budgets, forecasts, and accounting documentation

sites.name: claims-department
sites.description: Claims processing, case management, and claims documentation

sites.name: it-department
sites.description: IT policies, system documentation, and technology resources

sites.name: legal-compliance
sites.description: Legal documents, contracts, regulatory compliance, and policy documentation

sites.name: marketing-department
sites.description: Marketing campaigns, brand assets, and promotional materials

sites.name: sales-department
sites.description: Sales resources, client information, and sales documentation

sites.name: operations-department
sites.description: Operational procedures, process documentation, and efficiency initiatives

sites.name: customer-service
sites.description: Customer support resources, service documentation, and support tools

sites.name: company-announcements
sites.description: Company-wide news, announcements, and organizational updates

sites.name: employee-intranet
sites.description: Central hub for all employees - news, resources, and company information

sites.name: training-development
sites.description: Employee training materials, learning resources, and professional development

sites.name: health-safety
sites.description: Health and safety policies, procedures, and compliance documentation

sites.name: project-management-office
sites.description: PMO resources, project templates, and methodology documentation

sites.name: research-development
sites.description: R&D projects, innovation initiatives, and product development documentation

sites.name: quality-assurance
sites.description: QA processes, testing documentation, and quality standards

sites.name: facilities-management
sites.description: Building management, maintenance schedules, and facility resources

sites.name: corporate-communications
sites.description: Internal and external communications, press releases, and media resources

sites.name: employee-recognition
sites.description: Employee awards, achievements, and recognition programs

sites.name: social-committee
sites.description: Company events, social activities, and team building initiatives

_exclusions_instructions: Define email addresses and domains to EXCLUDE from being assigned as site owners or members.
This is useful when you (the admin running the script) don't want to be associated with the created sites.
Exclusions apply when using 'Random Azure AD' owner assignment mode.

exclusions._email_addresses_comment: Specific email addresses to exclude from owner/member assignment
exclusions._domains_comment: Domains to exclude - all users from these domains will be excluded
exclusions._patterns_comment: Wildcard patterns to exclude (supports * for any characters)
exclusions._log_exclusions_comment: Log when users are excluded (for visibility)

_deployment_tracking_instructions: A unique deployment ID is used to track which sites were created by this tool.
This ID is appended to site descriptions and used during cleanup to identify managed sites.
If deployment_id is empty, a random one will be auto-generated on first deployment.
Format: Looks like an internal reference code (e.g., 'PRJ-7X9K2M') - not identifiable as test data.

deployment_tracking._deployment_id_comment: Unique identifier for this deployment (auto-generated if empty)
deployment_tracking._append_to_description_comment: Append deployment ID to site descriptions for tracking
deployment_tracking._description_format_comment: How to append the ID to descriptions. Use {id} as placeholder.
